subject: Product Management
title: Amazon's One-Click Patent
description: A fast breakdown of Amazon's One-Click Patent for builders.

hook.text: Amazon tested one-click purchasing and found it massively increased conversion. They patented it.

buildup.text: The original checkout was multi-step: cart, shipping, payment, confirmation. Each step lost 20-30% of buyers.

discovery.text: One-click buying removed all friction: one button, purchase complete.

twist.text: The patent was so valuable that Apple licensed it for iTunes purchases.

climax.text: Amazon proved that removing even one unnecessary step in a purchase flow can be worth billions in revenue.

punchline.text: One click changed e-commerce forever. Every eliminated step is revenue saved.

quiz.question: What was the impact of Amazon's one-click purchasing?
options: It dramatically reduced cart abandonment by removing all checkout friction — so valuable it was patented
It had minimal impact on Amazon's sales
One-click was already standard before Amazon tested it
